# Qarote

> Qarote is a modern RabbitMQ monitoring and management dashboard for developers and DevOps teams. It provides a clean, intuitive interface to monitor queues, track performance, manage exchanges, and set up alerts across multiple RabbitMQ servers. Qarote replaces the default RabbitMQ Management Plugin with a faster, more powerful experience.

## Product Overview

Qarote is a SaaS product with a freemium model. The core monitoring tier is free and open source (MIT licensed). Premium features including multi-server support, workspaces, alerting, and integrations are available through paid plans. Qarote can be deployed as a cloud-hosted service or self-hosted on your own infrastructure. It supports RabbitMQ versions 3.x and 4.x, connects via the Management HTTP API over encrypted TLS connections, and works with both single-node and clustered deployments.

- Website: https://qarote.io
- Application: https://app.qarote.io
- GitHub: https://github.com/getqarote/Qarote
- Discord: https://discord.gg/GwHRbGwyUG
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/qarote/
- Contact: support@qarote.io
- Full details: https://qarote.io/llms-full.txt

## Features

### Live Queue Monitoring
Monitor queue depths, message rates, and consumer counts with live-updating charts. Track message accumulation and processing performance in real time across all your RabbitMQ servers.

### Smart Alerting System
Intelligent alerts for queue backlogs, memory usage, and performance issues. Customizable thresholds with severity-based notifications via email, Slack, browser notifications, and webhooks.

### Queue Management
Pause, resume, purge, and delete queues with one click. Create exchanges, bind queues, and manage routing keys through an intuitive interface. Full support for queue, exchange, virtual host, and RabbitMQ user management.

### Performance Analytics
Detailed metrics on memory usage, disk space, file descriptors, Erlang processes, and message throughput. Visualize trends with real-time charts and historical data.

### Multi-Server Support
Connect to multiple RabbitMQ clusters and switch between them seamlessly. Support for different environments (production, staging, development) and configurations.

### Message Publishing
Test and debug applications by publishing messages directly to queues and exchanges. Useful for development, integration testing, and troubleshooting.

### Topology Visualization
Visual representation of your RabbitMQ topology including queues, exchanges, and bindings.

## Pricing

### Community (Free)
- 1 RabbitMQ server
- 1 workspace
- 1 team member
- Basic statistics
- Queue, exchange, virtual host, and user management
- LTS RabbitMQ version support
- Community support
- No credit card required

### Developer ($29/month billed yearly, or $34/month billed monthly)
- 3 RabbitMQ servers
- 3 workspaces
- 3 team members
- Advanced statistics
- Alerts and webhooks
- Topology visualization
- All RabbitMQ versions (3.x and 4.x)
- Priority support

### Enterprise ($99/month billed yearly, or $124/month billed monthly)
- Unlimited RabbitMQ servers
- Unlimited workspaces
- Unlimited team members
- All Developer features
- SSO, SAML, and OIDC authentication
- SOC 2 compliance
- Free trial available

### Self-Hosted Pricing
- Community: Free (MIT licensed, via GitHub)
- Developer: $348/year
- Enterprise: $1,188/year

## How It Works

1. Sign up: Create an account in seconds with email or Google. No credit card required.
2. Add your servers: Connect your RabbitMQ servers with a simple connection string. Supports multiple environments and clusters.
3. Monitor and collaborate: Get real-time insights into queues, exchanges, and message flow. Monitor with your team and set up alerts.

## Comparison with Alternatives

### Qarote vs. RabbitMQ Management Plugin
The built-in RabbitMQ Management Plugin provides basic monitoring but is slow to load on large deployments, offers a cluttered interface, and only connects to a single broker at a time. Qarote provides a modern, fast interface that loads instantly even with thousands of queues, multi-server support, smart customizable alerts, real-time charts with historical data, and team collaboration with shared workspaces.

### Qarote vs. Prometheus and Grafana
Prometheus and Grafana are powerful general-purpose monitoring tools, but setting them up for RabbitMQ requires installing the Prometheus exporter plugin, configuring scrape targets, building custom Grafana dashboards, and maintaining the entire stack. Qarote provides purpose-built RabbitMQ monitoring with zero configuration -- connect your server and start monitoring immediately with pre-built dashboards, queue management controls, and alerting without writing a single PromQL query.

## Security

Connections to RabbitMQ servers use TLS encryption by default. Qarote is designed to read only the metrics and management data exposed by the RabbitMQ Management HTTP API -- it is not designed to store message payloads, credentials, or sensitive application data. Only configuration and alert preferences are persisted. SOC 2 compliance is available for Enterprise plans. Self-hosted deployment keeps data within your network.

## Deployment Options

- Cloud-hosted: Fully managed at app.qarote.io
- Self-hosted: Deploy on your own infrastructure via Docker, Docker Compose, Dokku, or standalone binary
- Self-hosted licenses use offline JWT key validation -- no phone-home required

## RabbitMQ Version Compatibility

- RabbitMQ 3.x: Fully supported (Community tier supports LTS versions only)
- RabbitMQ 4.x: Fully supported on paid plans
- Tested with single-node and clustered deployments
- Compatible with managed services: CloudAMQP, Amazon MQ

## FAQ

### What is Qarote?
Qarote is a modern RabbitMQ monitoring and management dashboard designed for developers and DevOps teams. It replaces the default RabbitMQ Management Plugin with a faster, more intuitive interface that lets you monitor queue depths, message rates, consumer counts, memory usage, and disk space in real time. Qarote supports RabbitMQ versions 3.x and 4.x, connects via the Management HTTP API over encrypted TLS connections, and works with both cloud-hosted and self-hosted RabbitMQ instances.

### Who is Qarote for?
Qarote is designed for developers, DevOps engineers, SREs, and platform teams who rely on RabbitMQ for message queuing and want better visibility into their broker infrastructure. Whether you manage a single RabbitMQ instance or operate dozens of clusters across environments, Qarote helps you spot queue backlogs before they cause outages, troubleshoot message routing issues faster, and keep your team aligned with shared dashboards and customizable alerts.

### Is Qarote secure?
Yes. Connections between Qarote and your RabbitMQ servers use TLS encryption by default. Qarote is designed to read only the metrics and management data exposed by the RabbitMQ Management HTTP API -- it is not designed to store message payloads, credentials, or sensitive application data. For teams with strict compliance requirements, the Enterprise plan includes SOC 2 compliance, and Qarote can be fully self-hosted to keep data within your network.

### How is Qarote different from the RabbitMQ Management UI?
The built-in Management Plugin is slow on large deployments, cluttered, and limited to a single broker. Qarote offers a modern fast interface, multi-server support, smart alerts via email/Slack/webhooks, real-time charts with historical data, and team collaboration with shared workspaces and role-based access.

### Is Qarote a better monitoring tool than Prometheus and Grafana?
Qarote and Prometheus/Grafana serve different purposes. Setting up Prometheus for RabbitMQ requires the exporter plugin, scrape configuration, and custom Grafana dashboards. Qarote provides purpose-built RabbitMQ monitoring with zero configuration. For teams already running Prometheus/Grafana, Qarote complements it with deeper RabbitMQ-specific insights and operational controls.

### Can I try Qarote for free?
Yes. The free Community tier includes 1 server, 1 workspace, and 1 team member with no time limit and no credit card. Upgrade to Developer ($29/month) or Enterprise ($99/month) when you need more. Self-hosted licensing starts at $348/year.

### Can I self-host Qarote?
Yes. Deploy using Docker, Docker Compose, Dokku, or standalone binary. The Community edition is free and MIT licensed. Self-hosted paid licenses use offline JWT validation -- no internet connection required after activation.

### What RabbitMQ versions does Qarote support?
RabbitMQ 3.x and 4.x, including latest releases. Connects via the Management HTTP API. Community tier supports LTS versions; paid plans support all versions. Tested with single-node, clustered, and managed services (CloudAMQP, Amazon MQ).

### Does Qarote work with RabbitMQ clusters?
Yes. Qarote provides cluster-aware monitoring, automatically discovering all nodes and displaying node-level metrics alongside cluster-wide aggregates. Multi-server support on paid plans lets you monitor multiple independent clusters from one dashboard.
